content:
- Read prior Ralph progress log and current/done task summaries.
- Confirmed actual repository stack is a Rust workspace (`Cargo.toml`, `Makefile`, crates under `crates/`) with a remaining vendored Go tree under `cockroachdb_molt/` that active tasks are targeting for removal/splitting.
- Next: refresh task summary, inspect active task files for `blocked_by` and `priority`, then write the selected task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and quit.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries, resolved active blockers against `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and inspected the live repository manifests plus README to confirm the actual stack is a Rust workspace (`runner`, `source-bootstrap`, `ingest-contract`) with a remaining Go verify tree under `cockroachdb_molt/`.
- Found no active failing bug tasks, no priority-tagged active tasks, and only one explicitly blocked task (`story-22a-runner-metrics/02` blocked by `story-22a-runner-metrics/01`). Story-16 tasks 01 and 03 already pass, while story-16 task 02 remains the closest unblocked architectural seam: it finishes removing runner-side verify behavior before the verify-only Go slice and image work proceed.
- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` to `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/02-task-remove-runner-side-verify-capability-and-code-paths.md`. Next context should start on that task using the `tdd` skill, then run `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` cleanly.

- Read prior progress, confirmed current task pointer selects story-16 task 02, and opened the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed this turn must branch on the task file state: planning only if it still says `TO BE VERIFIED`, execution only if it says `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: inspect the task file contents and either create/update the plan and quit, or execute the approved plan with TDD.

- Determined the task is still in planning state because the task file had no `<plan>` entry and no `NOW EXECUTE` marker.
- Read neighboring story-16 plan/task files to match the expected Ralph format: task file gets a `<plan>...</plan>` tag and the plan file ends with a status marker.
- Next: inspect remaining verify references in code, tests, and docs so the new plan targets the real residual runner-side verify boundary instead of duplicating task 01.

- Searched the repo for remaining verify references. The main runner crate already has the `verify` and `cutover-readiness` command/config surface removed, while the strongest remaining seams are in runner tests and integrity enforcement.
- Confirmed story-16 task 03 already owns the SQL-only source bootstrap cleanup, so task 02 should stay focused on residual verify-specific harness/image/runtime enforcement and not absorb bootstrap work.
- Next: write a task-02 plan that replaces brittle file-string enforcement with typed/runtime contract checks and scopes out future story-18 verify-image work explicitly.

- Created the missing story-16 task-02 execution plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/02-task-remove-runner-side-verify-capability-and-code-paths_plans/2026-04-19-runner-verify-removal-plan.md` and linked it from the task file.
- The plan is scoped to residual runner-side verify cleanup and enforcement only: centralize forbidden verify markers in shared test support, tighten E2E integrity/harness boundaries, keep the runner image/runtime contract destination-only, and explicitly avoid absorbing story-18 verify-image routing work.
- Next context should read the task file, see the linked plan marked `NOW EXECUTE`, then execute it strictly with red-green TDD and finish all required lanes (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`).

- Read progress log, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the previous context already created a plan for story-16 task 02 and the next step is to inspect task/plan state to decide execution vs immediate quit.
- Next: read the task file and linked plan, inspect worktree status, then execute the approved plan with vertical red-green slices if it is marked NOW EXECUTE.

- Executed the first red-green TDD slice for story-16 task 02.
- RED: changed `crates/runner/tests/cli_contract.rs` to depend on a new shared `runner_public_contract` support module, then confirmed the targeted test failed to compile because the file did not exist.
- GREEN: added `crates/runner/tests/support/runner_public_contract.rs` with canonical runtime subcommands and forbidden removed-surface markers; `cargo test -p runner runner_help_lists_only_destination_runtime_subcommands` now passes.
- Next: keep routing CLI/config/README/image-contract assertions through the shared public-contract boundary, then tighten the integrity support boundary before running repository lanes.

- Continued story-16 task 02 execution with shared public-contract refactors and targeted TDD.
- Routed CLI/config/README and the long-lane image validate-config assertions through `crates/runner/tests/support/runner_public_contract.rs`, then flattened that helper to one reusable removed-surface assertion so the test binaries compile without dead-code/import noise.
- RED/GREEN on integrity cleanup: changed `crates/runner/tests/e2e_integrity_contract.rs` to depend on a new `support/e2e_integrity_contract_support.rs`, confirmed compile failure because the support module did not exist, then added `E2eIntegrityContractAudit` and got `cargo test -p runner --test e2e_integrity_contract e2e_suite_no_longer_routes_integrity_through_runner_verify` green.
- Also re-ran targeted public-contract tests: cli help, config validate, README destination-only contract, and the ignored long-lane image test all passed.
- Next: finish moving the remaining integrity file-scan/bypass/raw-docker audits into `E2eIntegrityContractAudit`, then run the full required lanes (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`) and update the task file only if every lane passes.

- Finished the required verification lanes for story-16 task 02: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed.
- Completed a final improve-code-boundaries pass by collapsing duplicated removed-verify assertions into `runner_public_contract` support and moving repo-layout integrity audits into `e2e_integrity_contract_support`, reducing stringly checks in top-level test files.
- Updated the task file status to completed, checked every acceptance criterion, and set `<passes>true</passes>`.
- Next: run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, clean out accidental untracked `.DS_Store` junk from the workspace, stage only the task deliverables plus required Ralph state files, commit with the required task-finished message, push, and quit.
